Linear Models Report - Experiment 3 Sub-2 Run 1

Generated 2026-04-12 07:25  |  Train: 2021-2024  |  Test: 2025
Purpose: Evaluate three linear models - OLS (unregularised baseline), Ridge (L2), and ElasticNet (L1+L2) - on Experiment 3 Sub-2 datasets (Exp2 Sub-2 baseline + ADD-tier + CONSIDER-tier candidate features per target). CONSIDER-tier features add higher marginal row cost (up to 35-50%) but broader potential signal coverage. All feature sets use StandardScaler. Ridge α and ElasticNet α/l1_ratio are tuned via GridSearchCV + TimeSeriesSplit(n_splits=3).
Selection criterion for “best model” cards: Win count - the number of individual datasets on which a model achieves the highest Test R² (ties go to the first model that reaches the maximum). Average Test R² is shown separately but not used for selection, because OLS produces catastrophically negative R² values on some datasets that would unfairly distort its mean.
OLS (unregularised)
Ridge (L2, tuned α)

Experiment 3 Sub-2 - Exp2 Sub-2 Baseline + ADD + CONSIDER-tier Features run 1

Feature count: 25  |  Datasets: 8  |  Train: 2021-2024  |  Test: 2025

Best model: ElNet wins on Test R² in 6/8 datasets in this experiment.
Average Test R²: OLS -0.103 | Ridge +0.331 | ElNet +0.351.
Generalisation gaps: OLS shows high overfit (avg gap +0.77); Ridge shows high overfit (avg gap +0.25).
